items.title: There are no survivors in the blast at a Tennessee explosives factory, sheriff says
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The blast in rural Tennessee that leveled an explosives plant and was felt for miles around left no survivors, authorities said Saturday. The total number of people who died was unclear, as was the cause of the blast. (AP video: Obed Lamy)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: US special envoy Witkoff and US president's son-in-law Kushner meet military officials in Middle Eas
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff and President Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner met with senior U.S. and Israeli military officials in the Middle East on Saturday.

items.title: Paul Mescal and Jessie Buckley bring 'Hamnet' to London
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Paul Mescal, Jessie Buckley, and director Chloé Zhao's premiere their anticipated adaptation of Maggie O’Farrell’s bestseller, "Hamnet," at the BFI London Film Festival. (Oct. 11)

items.title: Norwegians protest against Israeli soccer team playing World Cup qualifiers
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to a stadium in Oslo where a soccer World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel was set to be played on Saturday. (AP video shot by: Kostya Manenkov)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Russia’s top official attends military parade in North Korea and meets Kim Jong Un
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Deputy chairman of Russia’s National Security Council, Dmitry Medvedev, met with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un during his visit to Pyongyang to attend a massive military parade marking the 80th anniversary of the ruling Workers’ Party.

items.title: 2 dead after shooting on school campus in Mississippi
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Police in a small Mississippi town are investigating a shooting during the school’s homecoming weekend that left two people dead.

items.title: Tear gas and arrests in Abidjan as opposition defies protest ban
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Police in Ivory Coast used tear gas on Saturday to disperse small groups of protesters who had defied a ban on an opposition march in Abidjan.

items.title: AP explains Iran's position after Gaza ceasefire
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>As the Middle East broadly welcomes a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war, Iran finds itself at one of its weakest moments since its 1979 Islamic Revolution. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: France's newly reappointed prime minister says there weren't 'a lot of candidates' for his job
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>France's newly re-appointed prime minister acknowledged Saturday that there weren't ''a lot of candidates'' for his job and that he might not last long in the post given the country's deep political divides. (AP video shot by Philippe Marion)


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Drone video shows massive destruction in southern Gaza City
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israel’s bombardment of Gaza has left large swathes of the Strip in ruins, including parts of Gaza City which was the focus of Israel’s offensive before the ceasefire.

AP drone footage taken on Saturday showed massive destruction of the city’s Tal al-Hawa neighborhood.

#shorts #gaza #drone #footage</article>

items.title: Displaced Palestinians pass scenes of devastation in Gaza City as they return to their homes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of Gaza City residents who fled their homes to safer areas in central and southern Gaza have returned only to find what they once called homes reduced to rubble. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Students destress with one-on-one cat time in New York
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>New York university students are getting a purr-fect chance to unwind and destress this week with a mobile cat pod designed for some one-on-one with a cat.

It's all part of the "Cats as Therapy" project where each person gets about 10 minutes to sit with a cat.

#cats #therapy #newyork #shorts</article>

items.title: AP explains North Korea military parade to mark 80 years of ruling Workers' Party
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
